schema.description: The unique identifier for an OAuth client.
schema.title: OAuth Client Identifier

success.description: Whether the API call was successful.
Whether the API call was successful.

DELETE /accounts/{account_id}/oauth_clie.description: Removes the old client secret after a rotation, keeping only the new one. Use this after you have updated your client configuration to use the new secret. The `has_rotated_secret` field on the client indicates whether there is an old secret to delete.

schema.description: The unique identifier for an OAuth client.
schema.title: OAuth Client Identifier

success.description: Whether the API call was successful.

200.description: Delete Rotated OAuth Client Secret response

success.description: Whether the API call was successful.

4XX.description: Delete Rotated OAuth Client Secret response failure

GET /accounts/{account_id}/oauth_clients.description: List all OAuth clients for an account.

success.description: Whether the API call was successful.

count.description: Total number of results for the requested service

page.description: Current page within paginated list of results

per_page.description: Number of results per page of results

total_count.description: Total results available without any search parameters

allOf.description: Fields shared by OAuth client responses and create/update requests.

client_name.description: Human-readable name of the OAuth client.

client_uri.description: URL of the home page of the client.

grant_types.description: Array of OAuth grant types the client is allowed to use. `authorization_code` is required; `refresh_token` may be included optionally.

policy_uri.description: URL that points to a privacy policy document.

post_logout_redirect_uris.description: Array of allowed post-logout redirect URIs.

redirect_uris.description: Array of allowed redirect URIs for the client.

response_types.description: Array of OAuth response types the client is allowed to use.

scopes.description: Array of OAuth scopes the client is allowed to request. Colon-delimited scopes are not accepted. Dot-delimited scopes are validated against available OAuth API scopes; simple identity scopes are allowed. Protocol scopes `offline_access` and `openid` are added or removed automatically based on `grant_types` and `response_types`.

token_endpoint_auth_method.description: The authentication method the client uses at the token endpoint.

tos_uri.description: URL that points to a terms of service document.

allOf.title: OAuth Client Common Fields

allOf.description: The unique identifier for an OAuth client.
allOf.title: OAuth Client Identifier

client_uri_verification.description: Client URI domain control verification state.

status.description: Current verification status for the client URI host.

text.description: Exact TXT record value that must be added to DNS to prove ownership of the client URI host.

client_uri_verification.title: OAuth Client URI Verification

created_at.description: Timestamp when the OAuth client was created.

has_rotated_secret.description: Indicates whether the client has a rotated secret that has not yet been deleted.

promoted_at.description: Timestamp when the OAuth client was promoted to public visibility.

updated_at.description: Timestamp when the OAuth client was last updated.

success.description: Whether the API call was successful.

GET /oauth/scopes.description: List all available OAuth scopes. This endpoint requires authentication but has no authorization role requirements.

success.description: Whether the API call was successful.

count.description: Total number of results for the requested service

page.description: Current page within paginated list of results

per_page.description: Number of results per page of results

items.description: An available OAuth scope that can be assigned to an OAuth client.

category.description: Category for grouping scopes in the UI.

id.description: The scope label to use in the scopes array when creating or updating an OAuth client.

name.description: Human-readable name of the OAuth scope.

scopes.description: The underlying resource scopes (Bach scopes) that define which resources this OAuth scope can act upon.

items.title: OAuth Scope

visibility.description: Promote the OAuth client from private to public visibility. Only `public` is accepted; demotion to `private` is not supported. Promotion requires a non-empty client name, logo URI, verified client URI host, and at least one non-identity scope.

POST /accounts/{account_id}/oauth_client.description: Creates a second client secret so you can update your client configuration before deleting the old one. The `has_rotated_secret` field on the client will be set to `true`.
